place: Duanesburg CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Duanesburg named after James Duane, a New York jurist and Continental Congress delegate.
The hamlet grew around the Delaware and Hudson Railroad in the mid‑19th century, turning it into a key stop between Albany and Binghamton.
In 1907 Delanson, a nearby hamlet, hosted the world’s largest coaling station on the Delaware and Hudson’s Susquehanna Division, a major refueling point until 1929.
The town’s historic core features 643 structures recognized by New York State, plus 55 cemeteries and natural sites like Christman Sanctuary and Featherstonaugh State Forest.
Duanesburg sits at the intersection of U.S. Route 20 and New York State Route 7, about 25 miles northwest of Albany on the Allegheny Plateau.
Early settlers, mainly German and Scottish families, arrived in the late 18th century, filling all major farm lots by 1797 and shaping the town’s agricultural heritage.
